mnemonics.title: Loop Types记忆
mnemonics.content:
FWD - For, While, Do-while
For: Fixed iterations
While: Condition first
Do: Do it, then check condition

acronyms.expansion: For Off Repeat (loop counter starts at initial value, repeats until condition fails)
acronyms.usage: Used when number of iterations is known beforehand

acronyms.expansion: When Hooked In Loop Execution (loop keeps executing while condition is TRUE)
acronyms.usage: Used when iteration depends on runtime condition

acronyms.expansion: Do Once Then Evaluate (do the work, then check if continued)
acronyms.usage: Menu-driven programs, guaranteed at least one execution

visualTips.visualization: Draw three boxes: init → condition → increment, with arrows forming a circular flow back to condition
Draw two flowcharts: while shows decision diamond BEFORE process box; do-while shows decision diamond AFTER process box
Draw two concentric circles: outer loop = larger circle, inner loop = smaller circle, showing inner completes for each outer iteration

analogies.analogy: Like counting laps on a track - you know exactly how many laps to run before starting
Like waiting at a traffic light - you keep checking (looping) until it turns green (condition becomes false)
Like taking a test and then deciding whether to review - you do it once before evaluating
Like hitting the emergency stop button on a machine - immediately exits the operation
Like skipping one question on a test and moving to the next one - doesn't exit the entire test
